title: audit-test structured emission (v0)
description: The machine-readable batch provenance tally `/audit-test --emit-json` writes for Gate to ingest (the B→A graduation, TzolkinB/skills#49). It carries only the per-class COUNTS — the model's per-test judgment (which test is hollow) crystallised into numbers. Gate DERIVES the category (verdict.result + label) from these counts mechanically, exactly as it restates Playwright's `stats`; the gate downstream reads only the derived category, never these counts (honesty guard #1). Deriving the label at ingest is what makes the theater guard structural: deepAudited=0 derives `unexamined`, which can never reach `ship`.

scope.description: informational — what was audited, e.g. "--changed", a glob, or a file path

deepAudited.description: tests that advanced past triage to a mutation run

provenHollow.description: 🔴 proven false-confidence (execution-proven; mutation survived, reachability confirmed)

likelyHollow.description: 🟡 likely false-confidence (reasoned only — env not runnable / stale harness)

baselineLock.description: ⚠️ baseline-lock suspected (live assertion pinned to a regressed value)

unexamined.description: triaged clean, never mutated — NOT evidence of health

$comment: Gate derives: result = provenHollow>0 ? FAILED : (likelyHollow>0 || baselineLock>0) ? WARNED : PASSED. label = (provenSolid>0 || provenHollow>0) ? proven : deepAudited>0 ? likely : unexamined. ship-eligible ⟺ result=PASSED AND label=proven. NO confidence/score field — its arrival is the signal calibration has landed (schemaVersion bump). INGEST GUARDS (#111): `schema` is matched EXACTLY (a model produced this — a bogus `witness-audit-test/v999` is rejected, not accepted by prefix), and the counts must be internally consistent or the whole emission is rejected (degrades to opaque/absent, never a silent upgrade): audited === deepAudited + unexamined, and provenSolid+provenHollow+likelyHollow+baselineLock ≤ deepAudited. So `{provenSolid:1, deepAudited:0}` can never derive `proven`.
